name: Create research-based LinkedIn posts with OpenAI, Perplexity and human review

values.content:
=🎯 Your task is to generate a **visual image prompt** for creating a **conceptual infographic** that will visually support a LinkedIn post.

The image should clearly and attractively illustrate the emotion, concept, or core message of the post — using an **abstract but readable design**, ideal for grabbing attention during scrolling.

---

📄 POST CONTENT:  
{{ $('Content Aggregator').item.json.message.content }}

---

✅ DESIRED VISUAL STYLE:
- Abstract or conceptual infographic
- Vector graphics, geometric shapes, minimal layouts
- High contrast, clean lines, professional color palette
- Readable and catchy, made to stand out in a LinkedIn feed

✅ YOU CAN INCLUDE:
- Names of tools (e.g., n8n, OpenAI, ChatGPT), in **stylized/visual form only**
- Icons, symbolic representations, or simplified elements
- Flow-based visuals, arrows, automation concepts, productivity metaphors

🚫 AVOID:
- Hyper-realistic photography
- Designs that are too abstract to understand
- Detailed software interfaces or pixel art
- Long text (labels like “AI”, “Flow”, “Data” are fine)

---

🧠 VISUAL APPROACH SUGGESTIONS (pick one that fits best):
- **Automation/Workflow:** Stylized flowchart with curved arrows, tool icons (e.g., n8n + OpenAI), few bold colors  
- **Productivity/Efficiency:** Central engine (e.g., AI) triggering smaller parts, clean connected shapes  
- **Small Team, Big Impact:** Small node with large radiating influence, scalable bar graphs  
- **Tool Integration:** Puzzle of flat-style icons (e.g., Gmail, Notion, Zapier) fitting together visually

---

🎨 GRAPHIC STYLE REQUIREMENTS:
- Vector, flat design or semi-isometric
- 2–3 primary colors, professional and clean
- No long text or realistic 3D
- Balanced, center-weighted composition

---

✏️ OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return **only the final image generation prompt** (ready to use in DALL·E or similar). No further explanation.

---

**Prompt:**

parameters.content:
🕒 WORKFLOW STARTER
This triggers the content generation process automatically.

Setup Required:
• Set your preferred trigger interval. 

Below I suggest two possible trigger. If you want to automate your workflow, change the trigger with the schedule.

message.content:
=Research the topic: {{ $json.body.Topic }}

Additional context: {{ $json.body.Additional_Context }}

I’m researching **AI Agents and Large Language Models (LLMs)** with the goal of creating high-quality, factual content focused on **practical applications in the workplace**.

Please return only **verifiable, recent information** (from 2024–2025) and structure the response around these points:

1. Summarize **recent news, trends, or innovations** in the space — include **exact dates** and **direct source URLs**.
2. Highlight **concrete business use cases** or deployments — focus on **measurable results** and clearly defined workplace benefits.
3. Identify **high-signal discussions** on platforms like Reddit, X, or reputable forums — ideally with links to original threads.
4. Include **expert opinions or statements** from industry professionals or research-backed reports — ensure sources are cited.

---

📌 **CRUCIAL RULES:**
- Only include **verified facts** with **direct URLs** to sources.
- **No vague claims**, unlinked stats, or general AI hype.
- If data is unavailable or unverifiable, explicitly write: *"No recent verifiable information found."*
- If a source is outdated or lacks context, state the limitation.

---

🎯 **DELIVERABLE:**
Provide a concise list of **exactly 3 key insights**, clearly labeled and sourced. Use bullet points. No filler.

---

🚫 **Do NOT include:**
- Speculative content
- Marketing fluff or overly technical research with no workplace application
- Sections titled “Content Angles” or “Verified Insights” — just go straight to the findings.

---

🧱 **FORMAT Example:**
1. **[Insight Title]**  
   - Summary of the finding  
   - Source: [URL]  
   - Date: [Month YYYY]

2. …

If nothing meaningful is found for a point, write: *"No recent verifiable information found."*

parameters.content:
🧠 Perplexity Research – Trends & Use Cases

This node queries the Perplexity API to retrieve verified, up-to-date insights on a specific topic — ideal for identifying real-world trends, use cases, and expert opinions.

✅ Why Perplexity?
Among available LLMs, Perplexity offers the most source-driven answers, making it the top choice for factual content creation.

⚙️ Setup Required

Create a Perplexity API account and set your API key in the credentials.

✏️ How to Personalize

Open the prompt inside the node and:

Change the main topic you want to research (e.g., switch from AI Agents to AI in Healthcare)

Adjust the number of insights (default = 3)

📨 Gmail – Select the Best Topic (Human-in-the-loop)

This node sends you an email listing the 3 main topics extracted from the previous Perplexity node. You act as the human-in-the-loop, choosing which topic to use for your content.

✅ Why Gmail?
It's quick, reliable, and always accessible — whether you're on your phone or laptop.

⚙️ Setup Required

Connect your Gmail account

Set your email address as the recipient

✏️ How to Personalize

Customize the email subject and body to fit your tone or workflow

Decide what information to display (currently: topic titles only)

Optionally, replace Gmail with Telegram or Slack for faster interaction

parameters.subject: Perplexity Research – Trends & Use Cases

parameters.content:
🗂️ Legend – Node Color Coding
To help navigate and document this workflow, notes are color-coded based on their function:

🔵 Blue – General information or high-level context
🟢 Green – Workflow triggers
🟡 Yellow – Standard processing nodes
🔴 Red – Nodes that require API integration and may incur execution costs

Use this legend as a reference before diving into the detailed node explanations.

🧠 OpenAI – Content Creation Support

This is one of the core nodes of the workflow. It generates the actual LinkedIn post based on the topic you selected and the insights retrieved earlier.

Here, you can review and personalize the prompt to reflect your personal information, tone of voice, and the desired output language (currently set to Italian for my audience).

⚙️ Setup Required

You’ll need an OpenAI API key (note: this is different from a ChatGPT account)

⚠️ API calls have a cost, so choose your model wisely
→ I suggest balancing performance and price — you're writing a LinkedIn post, not launching a rocket 🚀

✏️ How to Personalize

Open the prompt inside the node and replace my personal details with your own

You can customize:

The writing style (e.g., tone, use of emojis, structure)

The language of the output

For an extra check, paste the prompt into ChatGPT and refine it there based on your style

values.content:
=You are helping write a **LinkedIn post** for **Alberto Bordoni**, an Assistant Manager with a strong background in **Data, Artificial Intelligence, and Program Management**. He's also a **trainer and YouTube content creator**, passionate about productivity, automation, and practical AI applications in the workplace.

---

🔎 **TOPIC to focus on:**  
{{ $json.data['Quale vuoi approfondire? '] }}

---

📥 **RESEARCH INSIGHTS (use only if directly relevant to the topic):**  
{{ $('🔍 Research the Trends').item.json.choices[0].message.content }}

---

🗣️ **LANGUAGE OF THE OUTPUT:**  
Please write in **{{ $json.data['Language'] || 'Italiano' }}**  
(The user can change this input in the node manually)

---

🎯 **CONTENT OBJECTIVES:**  
Your goal is to write a **medium-length LinkedIn post**, based on Alberto's **personal perspective and real experiences**, using a **human, thoughtful, and slightly ironic tone**. You can include short personal anecdotes or reflections that tie directly to the chosen topic.

The tone should feel **authentic, not corporate** — as if Alberto is speaking directly to his audience, sharing lessons or thoughts rather than teaching or preaching.

---

✅ **KEY CREATION GUIDELINES:**

1. **Start from personal experience** – avoid opening with research or generic statements  
2. **Keep it real** – write like a human: warm, curious, and conversational  
3. **Include 1–2 relevant research points** only if they support the main story  
4. **Add emojis sparingly** – only if they help with readability  
5. **Use headings or bold for readability** (especially for web/email display)

---

🚫 **AVOID:**

- Making research the main character – Alberto’s voice should lead  
- Generic “tips” or lists without context  
- Aggressive or overly directive tone  
- Overuse of buzzwords  
- Fabricating data or quotes

---

🔧 **WRITING PROCESS**

**Step 1: Build the main narrative**  
- Use the topic as your starting point  
- Frame it around a real thought, reflection, or question Alberto might have  
- Include concrete details or moments from his experience  

**Step 2: Enrich with relevant research**  
- Pick 1 or 2 key data points to validate the message  
- Insert them smoothly with phrases like:  
  - “Recent data shows…”  
  - “Interestingly, this aligns with…”  

**Final Tip:**  
Make sure it sounds like something Alberto would actually say — smart but approachable, grounded in real work, and a bit witty when it fits.

---

🧪 BONUS: Try to make it feel **undetectable as AI-generated**.  
Write as if you’re helping a real person put their thoughts into words — not generating a piece from scratch.

parameters.content:
## 🚀 Automate LinkedIn Content Creation with Perplexity, OpenAI & Gmail

What This Workflow Does:
- Generates original, research-based LinkedIn posts
- Combines AI insights with personal storytelling
- Includes human-in-the-loop selection & revision steps
- Automatically creates a conceptual image via DALL·E 3
- Sends the final post and image via email, ready to publish

Perfect For:
- Professionals who want to share high-quality AI-assisted content
- Content creators balancing consistency and authenticity
- Consultants and solopreneurs building a personal brand
- Anyone who wants to turn AI research into personal, sharable stories

## 📋 WORKFLOW PROCESS OVERVIEW

Step 1: 🔍 Perplexity finds 3 recent, verifiable AI-related topics  

Step 2: 📧 Email sent – you choose your favorite topic  

Step 3: ✍️ OpenAI generates a LinkedIn post draft  

Step 4: 📨 You review the post and approve or suggest changes 
 
Step 5: 🛠️ If needed, AI revises the post based on your feedback  

Step 6: 🎨 DALL·E creates a conceptual image to match the content  

Step 7: 📬 Final email sent – post text + image ready to copy-paste on LinkedIn

📝 Edit Fields – Content Aggregator & Finalizer
This node collects and consolidates the final version of the content before it's published or sent.

While it may look simple, it's strategically important because it:

💬 Receives the original content from the OpenAI node

🧠 Will also receive updated content from a future node designed to review and refine the post

⚙️ Setup Required

No specific setup needed — just ensure:

Input from OpenAI is correctly mapped

Future inputs (e.g., from the review process) are connected to overwrite or merge as needed

✅ Gmail – Content Review & Approval
This node sends the drafted LinkedIn post to your personal email, allowing a final manual review and approval step.

In the email, you'll find the full content generated by OpenAI.
You’ll be asked: "Do you like the content?"

If your answer is Yes, the workflow proceeds automatically
If No, you can suggest improvements using a form in the next node — those suggestions will be used to refine the content

⚙️ Setup Required

Connect your Gmail account
Set your personal email as the recipient
Ensure the message content is correctly mapped from the Edit Fields node

✏️ How to Personalize

Customize the subject and message tone to fit your voice
You can modify the approval logic (e.g., based on keywords or reply tracking)
Optionally, add an alternative channel (e.g., Telegram or Slack) for faster interactions

parameters.message:
=Hi! 👋  
Your LinkedIn post on the topic you selected is ready!  
Here’s the draft — let me know what you think:

{{ $json.message.content }}

👍 If you like it, just reply with "Yes" and the workflow will continue.

✏️ If you'd like to make changes, you can suggest edits in the next step.
parameters.subject: Review LinkedIn Post

parameters.content:
🔀 IF – Content Approval Routing
This node acts as a decision point based on your reply to the Gmail approval email.

If the response is "Yes", the workflow proceeds to generate a custom image with OpenAI

If the response is not Yes (or left empty), it redirects to the Content Reviewer node for further editing suggestions

⚙️ Setup Required

Make sure the email reply content is captured correctly (e.g., via webhook or IMAP integration)

The condition should check for a clear "Yes" response — case-insensitive and trimmed

✏️ How to Personalize

You can modify the condition logic to accept variations like “Sure”, “Go”, or emoji replies 👍

🧐 OpenAI – Content Reviewer
This node helps you refine the LinkedIn post based on feedback, ensuring the final version is more aligned with your tone, goals, and personal voice.

It compares:

The original content generated earlier

The improvement suggestions you've entered manually in the previous step

Then it generates an updated version, keeping your writing style and storytelling priorities in mind.

⚙️ Setup Required

Connect this node to both:

The initial OpenAI content node

The manual feedback collection node

Make sure the structure of the prompt supports personalization

✏️ How to Personalize

Adjust the prompt to include more style instructions if needed

You can experiment with different OpenAI models (e.g., GPT-4 Turbo for longer reasoning)

values.content:
=You are helping **Alberto Bordoni**, an Assistant Manager with a strong background in **Data, Artificial Intelligence, and Program Management**, experienced in consulting, training, and content creation on productivity and AI at work.

Below, you’ll find two important pieces of content:

✍️ **Original post draft (to be improved):**  
{{ $('✍️ Content Creator').item.json.message.content }}

🛠️ **Feedback / improvement suggestions from Alberto:**  
{{ $json.data['Proposta di miglioramento'] }}

---

🎯 Your goal:
Revise the original post, implementing Alberto’s suggestions while staying aligned with his personal tone and style. The result should feel human, reflective, and personal — not generic or overly polished.

---

📘 **ABOUT ALBERTO (for writing context):**  
- Assistant Manager in a consulting firm, focused on Data & AI  
- Background in marketing, with a passion for program management and automation  
- Trainer and YouTube creator sharing insights on Excel, productivity, and AI  
- Works on complex digital transformation projects using AI-driven solutions  
- Interested in self-growth and techniques for working smarter

---

🧭 **CONTENT PRIORITIES:**  
1. **Share thoughts and reflections** – not a “how-to” or a lesson  
2. **Blend information with light irony** – human and insightful  
3. **Enrich with data only when it adds value**  
4. **Personal storytelling comes first** – use it as the main narrative anchor

---

📊 **RESEARCH DATA (use only if relevant to the selected topic):**  
{{ $('🔍 Research the Trends').item.json.choices[0].message.content }}

Use research as **support**, not as the central message.

**Ratio**:  
- Personal experience + reflections = 80%  
- Research data = 20%

Use phrases like:  
- “Recent data shows...”  
- “This aligns with insights from...”

---

🚫 **AVOID:**  
- Starting with stats instead of stories  
- Over-relying on research  
- Giving generic advice  
- Letting the research overshadow personal voice  
- Using a bossy or impersonal tone  
- Fabricating facts or quotes

---

✅ **MAKE SURE:**  
- The topic and context lead the content  
- Research is helpful but not dominant  
- Alberto’s unique voice and experience are front and center  
- Tone feels natural and genuinely written by a human

parameters.content:
🖼️ OpenAI – Image Prompt Generator
This node helps generate a custom image prompt based on the content of your LinkedIn post, using OpenAI’s image engine (DALL·E).

The image is meant to visually support the message of the post with an abstract infographic style that works well on LinkedIn.

✏️ How to Personalize

Edit the image prompt to match your visual preferences or message tone

Choose the best style based on your content type (workflow, integration, productivity, etc.)

You can fine-tune the balance between concept and clarity by adjusting color, shape, and complexity in the prompt

🧹 Code – Clean Image Prompt for API
This simple Code node cleans up the image prompt generated in the previous step to make it safe and compact for API calls (like OpenAI’s image generation).

⚙️ What it does

Escapes " characters

Removes \n and \r (line breaks / returns)

Trims unnecessary whitespace

Outputs a clean_prompt field, ready to use in the API

🧠 OpenAI – Generate Image (DALL·E 3)
This node takes the cleaned image prompt and uses OpenAI’s image generation model (DALL·E 3) to create a visual asset ready to be published with the LinkedIn post.

⚙️ Setup Required

An OpenAI API key with image generation access

The correct model selected (e.g., "dall-e-3" or "gpt-4-vision-preview" depending on API route)

Optional: adjust generation parameters (image size, quality, style, etc.)

Note: Image generation has a cost – use the API wisely

📬 Gmail – Final Delivery of Your LinkedIn Post
This node sends a summary email that contains your finalized LinkedIn post and the generated image. It’s the last step of the workflow — delivering everything you need in one place.

⚙️ Setup Required

Connect your Gmail account

Set your recipient address (your own or someone else’s for review)

Make sure the image URL or attachment is correctly mapped from the previous node

📦 What’s Included in the Email

✅ The final post content (text block ready to copy-paste)

🖼️ The generated image from DALL·E

✏️ How to Personalize

Adjust the email subject to reflect your brand or content type

parameters.subject: Ecco il tuo post completo per LinkedIN
